name: Popeye's Fried Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 package Italian Salad Dressing Mix, Powder
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first nine ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Put the cornflakes into another bowl.
3. Place eggs and cold water in a separate bowl.
4. Heat enough corn oil in a heavy roomy skillet to fill it 1 inch deep. Make sure the oil is very hot.
5. Grease a 9x12x2 baking pan with some of the oil.
6. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
7. Dip each chicken piece one at a time:
- First, into the dry coating mix.
- Then, into the egg and water mixture.
- Next, coat it with crushed cornflakes.
- Finally, return it to the dry mix.
8. Carefully place the chicken pieces in hot oil, skin-side down. Fry until browned for 3-4 minutes on medium-high heat. Turn and fry the other side until browned as well.
9. Do not overcrowd the pan during frying. Transfer the fried chicken to the prepared baking pan in a single layer, skin-side up.
10. Cover the pan with foil, sealing it on three sides while leaving one side open for steam to escape.
11. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 35-40 minutes, or until the chicken is thoroughly cooked and tender. Remove the foil and bake an additional 5 minutes to crisp the coating.
12. Serve 4 portions. Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 4 days but should not be frozen. The leftover coating mix (the first nine ingredients) can be stored at room temperature in a covered container for up to 2 months.
